note: Ground truth established by reading the corpus source directly (not from memory) — see TKI-64 audit. `expected` symbols are matched against a hit's `qname` by substring (case-sensitive) so either the bare method name or Class.method form counts as a match. `k` is the P@5 cutoff (top 5 hits scored).

questions.query: where do we retry failed requests
questions.note: httpx delegates retries to httpcore's connection pool via the `retries=` kwarg threaded through HTTPTransport/AsyncHTTPTransport __init__ and handle_request (_transports/default.py).

questions.query: how are cookies persisted across requests
questions.note: _models.py Cookies class: extract_cookies reads Set-Cookie from a response into the jar, set_cookie_header writes the jar onto an outgoing request.

questions.query: where is redirect loop protection / max redirects enforced
questions.note: _client.py _send_handling_redirects loops while response.has_redirect_location, raising TooManyRedirects once history exceeds max_redirects.

questions.query: how does httpx do HTTP digest authentication
where are default connection and read timeouts configured
questions.note: _config.py Timeout class (+ module-level DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0)); _client.py BaseClient.timeout property / _set_timeout apply it per-request. Broadened after first eval run surfaced Timeout.as_dict/__repr__/BaseClient.timeout as equally correct answers the original narrower list under-credited.

questions.query: how does httpx encode multipart form data for file uploads
questions.note: _multipart.py: encode_multipart_data is the entry point; MultipartStream/FileField/DataField build the multipart/form-data body. Broadened after first eval run surfaced FileField.render/__init__ and encode_multipart_data as equally correct answers the original narrower list under-credited.

questions.query: where does httpx decide the request method and headers to use after a redirect
questions.note: _client.py: _build_redirect_request composes the follow-up request; _redirect_method downgrades POST->GET on 303 etc.

questions.query: where do we retry failed requests
questions.note: downloadermiddlewares/retry.py RetryMiddleware retries on configured HTTP codes / exceptions, up to RETRY_TIMES.

questions.query: how are cookies persisted across requests to the same spider
questions.note: downloadermiddlewares/cookies.py CookiesMiddleware keeps a per-jobid CookieJar (meta['cookiejar']) across the crawl.

questions.query: where is redirect loop protection / max redirect count enforced
questions.note: downloadermiddlewares/redirect.py _redirect tracks redirect_times/redirect_ttl in request.meta and raises IgnoreRequest('max redirections reached') once exceeded.

questions.query: how does scrapy avoid crawling the same URL twice
questions.note: dupefilters.py RFPDupeFilter fingerprints each Request and drops repeats.

questions.query: where does scrapy check robots.txt before fetching a URL
questions.note: downloadermiddlewares/robotstxt.py RobotsTxtMiddleware defers requests until the robots.txt for the netloc is parsed, then calls RobotParser.allowed.

questions.query: how does scrapy adaptively throttle the download delay based on response latency
questions.note: extensions/throttle.py AutoThrottle adjusts a per-slot delay toward a target concurrency using measured latency.

questions.query: where are pending requests queued and prioritized between the memory and disk queues
questions.note: core/scheduler.py Scheduler splits requests between an in-memory priority queue and an optional on-disk queue (jobdir), backed by pqueues.py's ScrapyPriorityQueue/DownloaderAwarePriorityQueue. Broadened after first eval run surfaced the pqueues.py implementations as equally correct answers.
